channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Ship owner ordered to pay after 2022 crash sinks tugboats
item.description: A shipping company's attempt to avoid paying millions of dollars to Tasmania's port authority over the sinking of two tugboats in Devonport has been overturned by Australia's High Court.
item.dc:creator: Sandy Powell

media:group.media:description: TasPorts is trying to sue the owner of the Goliath for $22 million in compensation to cover the salvage, clean up and damage.

item.title: NT Police fraud squad seizes grenades, warheads in raids south of Darwin
item.description: Northern Territory Police have seized 32 explosive ordnance items, including hand grenades, rocket components and warheads in raids south of Darwin.

media:group.media:description: Police seized 32 explosive ordnance items suspected of having military origin, including munitions, hand grenades, rocket components and warheads in raids on properties south of Darwin last month.

item.title: 'Small comfort': Federal budget's $600m for Bondi terror attack response
item.description: The Executive Council of Australian Jewry has welcomed a $600 million federal budget funding commitment for the Jewish community, but says it is a "small comfort" for those who lost loved ones in the Bondi terror attack.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Corvo and Jessica Kidd

media:group.media:description: The federal budget includes more than $600 million to support Australia's Jewish community after the Bondi terror attack.

item.title: Patients of retired Sydney dentist advised to test for bloodborne viruses
item.description: Retired dentist William Tam may have exposed his patients to hepatitis B, hepatitis C and HIV at his clinic, according to NSW Health.

media:group.media:description: Patients may have been exposed to HIV, hepatitis B, or hepatitis C and are advised to get tested as a precaution.

item.title: Live: Ivan Cleary to step away from Panthers after 2027 NRL season
item.description: Penrith Panthers' four-time premiership-winning coach Ivan Cleary announces he will step aside as coach of the NRL club at the end of the 2027 season. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Jon Healy

media:group.media:description: Ivan Cleary has coached the Panthers in two stints — from 2012-15 and since 2019.

item.title: What's in the federal budget for older Australians?
item.description: Despite it being an intergenerational budget, specific parts of the economic strategy will impact older Australians.
item.dc:creator: Lewis Wiseman

media:group.media:description: There are several parts of the latest federal budget that will have an impact on older Australians.

item.title: Anxious prisoner sentenced for using tractor to escape from custody
item.description: The Queensland man's escape triggered a month-long manhunt that ended when he was found at a remote campsite near a rainforest.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Testa

media:group.media:description: Michael Graeme Rennie was serving a sentence of more than six years when he ran from Lotus Glen prison farm.

item.title: New report accuses Hamas of 'deliberately' using rape on October 7
item.description: A 300-page report from an Israeli non-profit organisation has detailed allegations of horrific sexual assault against victims of Hamas's October 7, 2023, attacks on Israel, accusing the group of "systematically and deliberately" using rape and sexual torture.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Doran

media:group.media:description: A woman visits the site of the Nova music festival where hundreds were killed and abducted by Hamas in 2023.

item.title: Here's how the budget's tax changes will affect you
item.description: Answers to all your questions about the federal budget's changes to negative gearing and the capital gains tax.
item.dc:creator: Tom Crowley

media:group.media:description: The federal government has announced major tax changes in the 2026 budget.

item.title: Terror accused charged with child abuse, extremist material offences
item.description: A man accused of planning terrorist attacks in Perth is charged with further offences, including possession of child abuse material and violent extremist material on his devices.
item.dc:creator: David Weber

media:group.media:description: Jayson Joseph Michaels has been charged with terrorism offences.

item.title: Police confirm body found in remote nature reserve is triple murderer
item.description: NSW Police has confirmed the body of a man found beside a vehicle in the state’s central west was that of triple murderer Julian Ingram.
item.dc:creator: Bruce MacKenzie

media:group.media:description: Police say the National Parks and Wildlife Service alerted them to an abandoned vehicle at the Round Hill National Park.

item.title: Adam Scott brings good form into 99th consecutive golf major
item.description: Adam Scott is being lauded for his "amazing" longevity as he prepares to tee up for a 99th consecutive major golf championship.

media:group.media:description: Adam Scott's remarkable run at golfing majors is on track to continue in Pennsylvania.

item.title: One Nation's win a concern for minorities living in Farrer
item.description: David Farley has won Farrer for One Nation in a landslide, but there are some in the community yet to be convinced of his ability to represent them.
item.dc:creator: Emily Doak and Nicola Ceccato

media:group.media:description: Braden Lyons says he is shattered by One Nation's success in Farrer.

item.title: Man on mobility scooter killed by car in Alice Springs
item.description: A 49-year-old man using a mobility scooter has died after being hit by a car on a pedestrian crossing in Alice Springs.
item.dc:creator: Tilda Colling and Stewart Brash

media:group.media:description: A man using a mobility scooter died in an accident in Alice Springs.

item.title: Government 'watching' as outback gold mine sets financial deadline
item.description: Queensland's largest gold mine has set a refinancing deadline, despite high global gold prices.
item.dc:creator: Meghan Dansie

media:group.media:description: The Sarsfield-Nolans pit at the Ravenswood mine.

item.title: Sammy the seal believed dead on Mornington Peninsula
item.description: A beloved seal known as Sammy is believed to have died, after Zoos Victoria confirmed they were called to a rescue on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ethan White

media:group.media:description: Sammy the seal was often spotted sleeping in several locations around Victoria's Mornington Peninsula.

item.title: Man granted bail over synagogue ramming had religious 'preoccupation', court hears
item.description: The 32-year-old was allegedly high on drugs when he rammed his car into the gates of Brisbane's largest synagogue in February.
item.dc:creator: Talissa Siganto

media:group.media:description: A man has been charged with offences including a hate crime after allegedly ramming the gate of the Brisbane Synagogue.

item.title: Federal police charge man with using social media to threaten Victorian MP
item.description: A man will face court after federal police charged him following an investigation into a social media post that allegedly threatened a federal politician.
item.dc:creator: Madeleine Stuchbery

media:group.media:description: Federal police have charged a man for allegedly threatening a federal MP on social media.

item.title: Solar eclipse to attract thousands to 'middle of nowhere' in 2028
item.description: One of the longest solar eclipses this century, albeit for five minutes, is set to put the remote East Kimberley on the world map.
item.dc:creator: Giulia Bertoglio

media:group.media:description: The 2028 total solar eclipse is expected to attract an extra 25,000 visitors to the East Kimberley.

item.title: 'An absolute victory': Good character evidence scrapped in NSW
item.description: A survivor advocate behind the campaign calling for good character references to be abolished in sentencing perpetrators of sexual offences has described the NSW reform as "an absolute victory".
item.dc:creator: Isabella Ross

media:group.media:description: Harrison James has spent the past four years tirelessly advocating to remove the provision of good character references when sentencing convicted paedophiles.

item.title: Eurovision pays musical homage to Austria-Australia confusion
item.description: The first semifinal of the Eurovision Song Contest includes a comedic musical interlude about the differences between Austria and Australia and sends 10 countries through to the grand final on Saturday.

media:group.media:description: Bzikebi, from Georgia, performs during the first semifinal of the 70th Eurovision Song Contest.

item.title: New search for SA mother missing from outback opal town
item.description: SA Police return to Andamooka to search for missing 41-year-old local woman Trisha Graf, who was last seen in December.

media:group.media:description: Trisha Graf was last seen on December 12.

item.title: Coronial inquest into death of tasered 95yo Clare Nowland begins
item.description: Three years after the great-grandmother was tasered by a police officer in her aged care home, an inquest is expected to focus on dementia training for aged care staff and emergency services.
item.dc:creator: Isla Evans and Adriane Reardon

media:group.media:description: Clare Nowland, who had symptoms of dementia, died in Cooma Hospital after being tasered.

item.title: Federal budget includes $50 million to duplicate Belconnen road
item.description: Other funding boosts for the ACT revealed in the budget papers include $50 million to duplicate Drake Brockman Drive in West Belconnen, aimed at servicing the area's growing population, and almost $10 million for the National Film and Sound Archive.
item.dc:creator: Niki Burnside

media:group.media:description: Belconnen, with Lake Ginninderra in foreground and Telstra Tower in distance.

item.title: Australia axes quick as Women's T20 World Cup squad is named
item.description: Brisbane Heat left-arm pace bowler Lucy Hamilton has been selected in Australia's T20 World Cup squad with the experienced Darcie Brown a surprise omission.

media:group.media:description: Darcie Brown has been left out of Australia's Women's T20 Cricket World Cup squad.
